jokes.text: What is it, one of those heaven can wait type of movies?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to guess the genre of a guest's movie without the guest wanting to discuss it
jokes.punchline: Larry names a specific, dated movie reference as a category
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's awkward interview style, but the reference feels slightly dated

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What is it, one of those heaven can wait type of movies?

jokes.text: No. No. [Laughter]
jokes.setup: Larry asks if the guest has hobbies
jokes.punchline: The guest's flat, immediate double negative
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. The guest's complete lack of elaboration is beautifully deadpan

dialogue.line: No. No.

jokes.text: Maybe if we knew that, we could figure out, Ah, it's a so-and-so doll. We'll work backwards.
jokes.setup: Larry tries to get the guest to reveal movie details through merchandising
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous logic of reverse-engineering a film from its potential toys
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist logic that feels very Larry - desperately trying to get information through convoluted means

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Maybe if we knew that, we could figure out, Ah, it's a so-and-so doll. We'll work backwards.

jokes.text: It's a story about a man who's finding himself.
jokes.setup: After all the dancing around, the guest finally reveals something about his movie
jokes.punchline: The most generic, clichéd movie description possible
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the buildup - after all the secrecy, it's the most banal description imaginable

dialogue.line: It's a story about a man who's finding himself.

jokes.text: Aw, bullshit. It was an excellent show.
jokes.setup: Cut to backstage after Larry's awkward interview performance
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately contradicts his own self-criticism with false bravado
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Larry's insecurity masked by false confidence

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Aw, bullshit. It was an excellent show.

jokes.text: Oh, you always do this when you're renegotiating your contract.

jokes.explanation: Good character insight and sets up the contract negotiation storyline effectively

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Oh, you always do this when you're renegotiating your contract.

jokes.text: Just remember, you've got the network by the balls. They can't do this job.

jokes.explanation: Establishes Stevie's character as overconfident and crude

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Just remember, you've got the network by the balls. They can't do this job.

jokes.text: Fucking little snot. He's too busy playing with his balls to shake my hand.

jokes.punchline: Artie's crude pun connecting the handshake slight to Stevie's earlier metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels and shows Artie's quick wit

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Fucking little snot. He's too busy playing with his balls to shake my hand.

jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Artie's old-school values vs. new Hollywood

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're always supposed to shake a man's hand.

dialogue.line: Ask me who the guests are tomorrow night. I'd tell you.
